text: BUSINESS INSURANCE FOR MICRO-ENTERPRISES. Many young entrepreneurs skip insurance, thinking it's only for big companies. But one fire, theft, or flood can completely destroy a startup with no savings. Types of business insurance relevant to micro-enterprises: (1) General Liability Insurance — covers third-party injuries or property damage at your business premises. Essential for food businesses, salons, and workshops. (2) Stock/Inventory Insurance — protects your goods against fire, theft, and natural disasters. Critical for shops, market vendors, and wholesale businesses. (3) Fire Insurance — covers premises and contents against fire damage. Important for businesses with flammable materials (welding shops, restaurants, fuel-related businesses). (4) Burglary/Theft Insurance — covers losses from break-ins and theft. Important for shops, phone repair businesses, and any business with valuable equipment. Major insurance providers in Uganda: Jubilee Insurance, UAP Old Mutual, ICEA LION, Sanlam, APA Insurance. Cost: typically 1-3% of the insured value per year. Example: insuring stock worth UGX 10,000,000 costs approximately UGX 100,000-300,000/year — a small price to protect your livelihood. Start with the coverage you need most (usually stock/inventory) and add more as your business grows.

HEALTH INSURANCE FOR SELF-EMPLOYED ENTREPRENEURS. As a self-employed youth, you have no employer-provided health cover. One serious illness or accident can bankrupt your business — medical bills of UGX 2-10 million are common for hospital admissions. Options for health coverage: (1) Community Health Insurance Schemes — organized at village/parish level, low premiums (UGX 20,000-50,000/month per person), covers basic healthcare at partner facilities. Ask at your local health centre. (2) Private Health Insurance — AAR Healthcare: plans starting from UGX 80,000/month, wide hospital network. Prudential Uganda: individual and family plans from UGX 50,000/month. Jubilee Health Insurance: comprehensive plans from UGX 100,000/month. (3) NHIS (National Health Insurance Scheme) — government scheme in development, expected to cover formal and informal sector workers. Cost range: UGX 50,000-200,000/month for basic individual coverage depending on the provider and plan level. Why this matters: One medical emergency can force you to sell business assets, borrow at high interest, or close your business entirely. Many young entrepreneurs have lost thriving businesses because they had no health cover. Pro tip: Start with a basic hospital-only plan and upgrade as your business grows. Some VSLAs and SACCOs offer group health insurance at reduced rates — ask your group leader.

VEHICLE INSURANCE FOR BODA-BODA AND TRANSPORT BUSINESSES. If you operate any motor vehicle for business, insurance is not optional — it's the law. Types of vehicle insurance: (1) Third-Party Insurance (MANDATORY) — covers injury or damage you cause to other people or property. Does NOT cover your own vehicle or injuries. Cost: UGX 100,000-200,000/year for motorcycles, UGX 150,000-300,000/year for cars and minibuses. Required for: all vehicles on public roads. Penalty for no insurance: UGX 500,000 fine or imprisonment. (2) Comprehensive Insurance — covers third-party liability PLUS damage to your own vehicle from accidents, theft, fire, and vandalism. Cost: UGX 300,000-600,000/year for motorcycles, UGX 800,000-2,000,000/year for cars. Recommended if: your vehicle is your primary income source and you cannot afford to replace it. PSV (Public Service Vehicle) requirements for passenger transport: PSV license, annual vehicle inspection, route permit, and passenger liability insurance. Why this matters for boda-boda riders: Accident liability can exceed UGX 50,000,000 if you injure someone seriously. Without insurance, you could face prison time and crushing debt. One accident without insurance can put you in debt for years. Third-party insurance costs less than UGX 20,000/month — far less than the risk of riding uninsured. Major vehicle insurers: Jubilee, UAP Old Mutual, ICEA LION, APA, Statewide Insurance.
